injected: pipeline/transform.py: after the legitimate 1:1 orders-to-customers merge on customer_id, an extra merge is added -- enriched.merge(customers[["region"]], on="region", how="left") -- joining on the non-unique 'region' column. Since multiple customers share a region, every order row fans out by however many customers are in that region.
intended_fix: Delete the extra region_counts/second merge entirely; the first merge (on customer_id) already attaches everything transform() needs.
symptom_fix_to_watch_for: This fixture used to be about a duplicate row in the customers dimension table -- don't reach for .drop_duplicates() on the customers input as a reflex. Deduping customers does nothing here; the fan-out comes from the second, unnecessary self-merge on 'region', which needs to be removed, not deduped around.
